item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well heck, those things could be anywhere. Time to make new glass?

I didn't shiver, get scared, or anything like that - but this article was odd. Not in a weird cheesy way, in a good, interesting, odd way. "You shouldn't be able to see us". What's that about? Makes you think about what those things were on the other side, and how much they know about us humans. I almost downvoted the article, but then I thought to myself "Well the author will probably explain or attempt to explain all this funky stuff." And you did! You did, author. Let me tell you, industrial factories are weird!

+1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
But was the glass even anomoulous though? It seems like the yellow guys are the anomaly, not the glass.

The "Hey! Yellow guy! Over here!" was jarring; it could be perceived as confrontational, and the SCP isn't going to want to aggravate an unknown entity unnecessarily.

Still, upvoted :).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What happened after the object had been neutralized?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: this heavily reminds me of 1218, both have yellow dudes and both have glass lol

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is a real good one. You should write a second scp about the yellow guys. This could be a good story arc

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Made me shiver. I really liked this article and the air of mystery it provides, although I agree with Laneous up there about the conversation appearing casual, and that it sounded like something from a D-class. In fact, I think it would've made more sense if a D-Class was in the car, instead of the Agent, because the Foundation has no clue what the entities are capable of, and would rather much have lost a D-Class and not an Agent. +1 still, though

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This reminds me a lot of 178, any connection there?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Pretty spooky, +1. I love how this implies that these yellow things are actually everywhere.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I lowkey guessed this was by you because like almost all of your SCPs are about cars.

In other news, this is pretty unnerving. My only gripe is that the agent's dialogue feels a little too casual? If it was a D-Class it might have made more sense.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Can you see the yellow people as well?

Thanks to DrLeibowitz and fitnessgram on #site19 for the critique.

Picture was taken by Wikipedia user Jagvar, and released to the public domain.
